title: Agro Preparation for Mimulus in Planta Transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4022f","text":"This protocol is part of a collection for Mimulus in planta transformation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":27,"length":47}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"0992A0B087DC11E9936523EAC2E12BB2","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/mi-3tkgnkw/protocols"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"40kiv","text":"See SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for warnings and hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate 4 agro colonies into separate 5 mL tubes of LB+Kan+Gent+Rif.
Colony PCR from the 5 mL culture to check that it has your insert.
Inoculate correct agro colony into a flask of 300 mL LB+Kan+Gent+Rif, and cover with foil.
Make glycerol stocks by adding 1 mL of agro to glycerol stock tube.
Transfer contents of flask into 500 mL centrifuge bottles and label them.
Balance the bottles (use the scale and spray bottle of water to balance).
Centrifuge to pellet the Agro. SPEED = TIME = 00:15:00 TEMP = 4 °C
While centrifuging, make the resuspension solution: 300 mL total for each construct 5% sucrose = 15 g sucrose 0.1 Molarity (M) acetosyringone = 2 mL/L = 600 µL
After centrifuging, pour off liquid into the original flasks the Agro was grown in, and bleach the flasks.
Resuspend the Agro pellet in 300 mL, resuspension solution by adding the solution to the centrifuge bottle and shaking.
Pour into spray bottle for infiltration.
Add 300 µL, Silwett to the Agro and shake to mix.
Shake for 12:00:00 to 16:00:00 at 28 °C - 30 °C . If left longer, the bacteria will begin to die.
Place in liquid nitrogen then store in -80 °C freezer.
Make acetosyringone fresh by dissolving 0.0196 g /mL in methanol.
Add dH 2 O to bring total volume to 300 mL , and mix.
creator: Andrea Sweigart
